ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Chitala ornata (featherback/belida). Mapped to closest parent freshwater fish food product class as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a species-level code for Chitala ornata. A0CJW (Freshwater fish) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for featherback fish (Chitala ornata). SNOMED CT coverage for Southeast Asian freshwater fish species is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of raw featherback fish is approximately 100 g, equivalent to a small fillet portion as commonly prepared in Southeast Asian households.

nutrient_source.title: Featherback (Belida), raw — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Featherback (Belida), raw (cr_id F03010015). Saturated fat and fatty acid fractions estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for raw freshwater fish (closest analogous species) as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions separately; see notes.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate fish
gi_source.source_match_notes: Featherback fish (raw) contains only 0.02 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No GI value is assigned. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (193 mg/100 g) offset by potassium (219 mg/100 g); favourable K:Na ratio supports blood pressure management
hypertension.guidance: Raw featherback fish provides 193 mg sodium and 219 mg potassium per 100 g, yielding a K:Na molar ratio above 1.0 consistent with DASH diet principles; patients with hypertension should avoid high-sodium preparation methods such as salting, pickling, or heavy soy sauce marinades, which can substantially increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.02 g/100 g) and high protein (16.88 g/100 g) with no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Featherback fish contains virtually no carbohydrate (0.02 g/100 g) and provides 16.88 g high-quality protein per 100 g, which slows gastric emptying and has no measurable glycaemic impact; it is suitable for blood glucose management per ADA dietary guidelines when prepared without batter, breading, or high-sugar sauces.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (1.2 g/100 g), low saturated fat (~0.3 g/100 g), and low cholesterol (36 mg/100 g) with lean high-quality protein
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Featherback fish is a lean white fish with low total fat (1.2 g/100 g) and low saturated fat (~0.3 g/100 g), making it a heart-healthy protein source consistent with AHA dietary recommendations; however, it lacks the high omega-3 fatty acid content of oily fish such as salmon or mackerel, so patients with CVD should also include omega-3-rich fish in their diet for additional cardioprotective benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lean white fish with calcium (75 mg/100 g) but lacking significant omega-3 fatty acids for anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Featherback fish provides calcium (75 mg/100 g) and high-quality protein but does not contain meaningful omega-3 fatty acids; it is not contraindicated in rheumatoid arthritis but should be supplemented with omega-3-rich foods such as salmon, walnuts, or flaxseed for stronger anti-inflammatory benefit, and should be prepared by steaming or grilling rather than deep-frying to avoid advanced glycation end-product formation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Featherback fish (Belida), known scientifically as Chitala ornata or Notopterus chitala, is a freshwater fish native to Southeast Asia, widely found in Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, and Thailand. In its raw state, it has silvery scales, white to grey firm flesh, and a flaky texture when cooked. It is a lean, high-protein fish providing approximately 78 kcal, 16.88 g protein, and only 1.2 g fat per 100 g, making it one of the lower-calorie freshwater fish options in the regional diet. Carbohydrate content is negligible at 0.02 g/100 g, rendering it suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Sodium is 193 mg/100 g in its raw form, which is moderate; however, sodium rises substantially when the fish is processed into fish balls, fish paste (otak-otak), or salted preparations. Potassium at 219 mg/100 g provides a favourable K:Na ratio supportive of blood pressure management. Calcium content (75 mg/100 g) is notable for a freshwater fish. Featherback is commonly used in Southeast Asian cuisine to make fish balls, fish cakes, and the popular Singaporean and Malaysian dish otak-otak. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, it is appropriate for patients managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when prepared without high-sodium or high-fat cooking methods.

Is featherback fish high in protein?
What is clown knifefish used for in cooking?
Is belida fish suitable for hypertension?
meal_context: raw ingredient for fish ball preparation

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F03010015) with confidence 0.95. Fatty acid fractions (saturated, monounsaturated, polyunsaturated) estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous freshwater fish entry and documented in notes. All other fields constructed from defensible sources.
notes: 1. FATTY ACID FRACTIONS: HPB Singapore Foodo does not report saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, or polyunsaturated_fat_g separately for this entry. Values of saturated_fat_g=0.3, monounsaturated_fat_g=0.3, polyunsaturated_fat_g=0.4 are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for raw freshwater fish (closest analogous species, NDB No. 15036 — Fish, carp, raw) as a proportional distribution of the reported total fat of 1.2 g/100 g. These are approximate and should be replaced with direct analytical data when available. 2. TRANS FAT: trans_fat_g set to 0.0 — confirmed negligible for raw unprocessed freshwater fish per USDA FoodData Central. 3. SUGAR: sugar_g set to 0.0 — raw fish contains no free sugars; confirmed negligible per HPB and USDA sources. 4. VITAMINS AND MINERALS: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for featherback (Chitala ornata) are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 5. GLYCEMIC INDEX: Featherback fish (raw) contains only 0.02 g carbohydrate per 100 g; glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. glycemic_index set to null, gi_category set to not_applicable. 6. ONTOLOGY: No species-level FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for Chitala ornata; closest_parent codes used as documented with gap notes. foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type set to closest_parent per allowed values. 7. CARBOHYDRATE NOTE: carbohydrate_g is 0.02 g/100 g — this is a naturally occurring trace amount in raw fish flesh, not zero; no zero-macro justification required as the value is non-zero.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'broader' to valid allowed values; set glycemic_index to null per critical rule for negligible-carbohydrate foods.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' (not in allowed list) to 'closest_parent' (allowed value per validator schema). Confirmed glycemic_index=null is correct numeric-type handling per q4 critical rule. All aliases_by_language entries verified present verbatim in flat aliases array.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on Q1-Q8. Core macros sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, confidence 0.95). Fatty acid fractions estimated from USDA analogous entry and documented. GI correctly set to null/not_applicable for negligible-carbohydrate fish. Multilingual coverage provided for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, Indonesian, and Thai. All three mandatory health conditions present with specific clinical guidance. Ontology anchors use closest_parent match type with honest gap notes — no codes invented. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Aliases are comprehensive and internally consistent.
